data.label: Watchtower
data.caption: Read PRs, checks, and Codex signals before acting.

data.label: Why this exists
data.text: ggg turns release-train muscle memory into typed tools: PR readiness, Codex review parsing, GOWORK=off validation, dependency-order merges, tag-patch releases, and Go proxy verification.
data.author: INFRA-001

data.definition: Green checks plus a satisfied Codex signal; no current-head inline feedback.

data.label: Signal
data.caption: Codex eyes means wait. Inline comments mean fix. Thumbs-up means proceed.

data.label: API map
data.text: GitHub GraphQL supplies statusCheckRollup, reviews, comments, reactions, head SHA, and inline review threads. The CLI should convert those raw shapes into stable ReadinessReport data.
data.author: Design guide

data.definition: Validation mode that proves published modules work without local workspace leakage.

data.label: Operator
data.caption: Batch watch stops when anything is actionable: ready, failed, feedback, or partial progress.

data.label: Release mill
data.caption: Dependency graph -> validate -> PR -> Codex -> merge -> tag -> verify proxy.

data.label: Intern path
data.text: Start with parity: port 01-pr-ready-check.py into Go with fixtures. Then add batch mode, module helpers, validation profiles, and release-train orchestration.
data.author: Next step

data.text: Do not sleep through actionable state. Turn signals into decisions, and decisions into safe commands.
data.author: ggg design rule
